auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants
economic_notes: Resource revenue sharing agreement with TAA from mining activities on N'Daki Menan earned $448,042. Multi-use facility became operational October 2019. Elder's Complex under construction, expected completion October 2020. OPP Station renovations underway. Daki Menan Lands and Resources Corporation operates fuelwood business, office/property rentals and forestry. $1.879 million in resource revenue proceeds held outside financial statements pending allocation decision.
notes: Auditor firm name not explicitly stated — identified only as Chartered Professional Accountants, Licensed Public Accountants based in North Bay, Ontario. Consolidated entity includes Daki Menan Lands and Resources Corporation (controlled non-profit). Long-term debt increased significantly in 2020 due to $5.532M loan advance (secured by General Security Agreement, matures June 2028) for capital construction. $1.879M in resource revenue proceeds held outside the financial statements pending community allocation decision — not consolidated. Contingent loan payable of $1,000,582 to INAC contingent on land claim settlement included in long-term debt. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC + Health Canada + Government of Canada + CMHC + Province of Ontario transfers ($5,640,200 + $1,783,316 + $862,643 + $155,332 + $2,592,809 = $11,034,300); OSR = $14,992,359 - $11,034,300 = $3,958,059. Note: OFNLP revenue of $1,028,399 included in OSR as it is not a direct government transfer.
